пБґ Acute hepatic porphyria (AHP) is a group of rare genetic
disorders that begin in your liver and go on to affect your
nervous system, causing symptoms throughout your body
пБґ Porphyria is a deficiency in one of the enzymes that your body
needs to make heme (a component of hemoglobin in your
blood, During the process of making heme, the absence of
specific enzymes causes other compounds to go unused and
build up in your body tissues

children.name: CAUSES
children.content:
пБґ Genetic mutation
пБґ Prescription and
recreational drug use
пБґ Excessive alcohol use
пБґ Severe calorie restriction
пБґ Stress
пБґ Anxiety
пБґ When liver does not have
the necessary enzyme to
metabolize another
byproduct

children.name: INTRODUCTION
children.content:
пБґ A condition in which the immune system reacts abnormally to a
foreign substance (allergen)
пБґ An exaggerated reaction by immune system to some harmless
substances (allergen) which normally does not affect most
people
пБґ Allergies, also known as allergic diseases, are a number of
conditions caused by hypersensitivity of the immune system to
typically harmless substances in the environment
пБґ These diseases include hay fever, food allergies, atopic
dermatitis, allergic asthma, and anaphylaxis
пБґ TYPES OF ALLERGY:
1. Drug or Medicine Allergy
2. Food Allergy
3. Insect Allergy
4. Latex Allergy
5. Mold Allergy
6. Animal (Pet) Allergy
7. Pollen Allergy

children.name: CAUSES
children.content:
пБґ By skin contact
пБґ Ingestion
пБґ Injection
пБґ Inhalation
пБґ Allergen

children.name: INTRODUCTION
children.content:
пБґ
а§Еа§Ъа§Ња§®а§Х а§Єа•З а§™а•А(cid:229)а§∞а§ѓа§°а•На§Є а§ђа§Ва§¶ а§єа•Л а§Ьа§Ња§®а§Њ, а§ђа§Ва§¶ а§Ѓа§Ња§єа§µа§Ња§∞а•А
пБґ ABSENCE OF MENSTRUAL PERIODS
пБґ Amenorrhoea is the absence of menstrual periods in a woman
during her reproductive years when women usually get periods
after puberty and before natural menopause
пБґ The natural cause of amenorrhoea are seen, most commonly,
during pregnancy and lactation (breastfeeding), the latter also
forming the basis of a form of contraception known as the
lactational amenorrhoea method
пБґ Outside of the reproductive years there is absence of menses
during childhood and after menopause
пБґ There are mainly two type of it based on condition of patient
пБґ 1. Primary amenorrhoea
вАҐ Menstrual cycles never starting may be caused by
developmental problems like
вАҐ Absence of uterus
вАҐ Ovarian failure
вАҐ Structural problem in female reproductive system
пБґ 2. Secondary amenorrhoea
вАҐ Normal Menstrual cycles but suddenly no periods due to
some reasons
вАҐ Menstrual cycles ceasing
вАҐ Hormonal disturbances by hypothalamus or pituitary
gland of brain
вАҐ It is defined as the absence of menses for three months in
a woman with previously normal menstruation or nine
months for women with a history of oligomenorrhoea
10

5. ANEMIA- а§Па§®а•Аа§ња§Ѓа§ѓа§Њ, а§Ца•Ва§® а§Х(cid:236) а§Ха§Ѓа•А
пБґ LOW HEMOGLOBIN (Hb)
пБґ Anemia is defined as a Low
number of red blood cells
пБґ In a routine blood test,
anemia is reported as a low
hemoglobin or hematocrit
пБґ Having anemia can make you
feel tired and weak
пБґ Condition called anemic
patient in which the blood
doesn't have enough healthy
red blood cells
пБґ Anaemia results from a lack
of red blood cells or
dysfunctional red blood cells
in the body
пБґ This leads to reduced oxygen
flow to the body's organs

PREVENTION
пБґ Iron: Iron-rich foods
пБґ Folate: This nutrient, and its synthetic form folic acid
пБґ Vitamin B-12: Foods rich in vitamin B-12 include meat, dairy
products, and fortified cereal and soy products
пБґ Vitamin C: Foods rich in vitamin C

пБґ Complete blood count (CBC)
пБґ A CBC is used to count the number of blood cells in a sample of
your blood, For anemia, the levels of the red blood cells

your blood need to study
пБґ A test to determine the size and shape of your red blood cells

children.name: INTRODUCTION
children.content:
пБґ
а§Па§Ьа§В а§Ња§За§®а§Њ а§™а•З(cid:179) а§Яа•Л(cid:229)а§∞а§Є ((cid:323)а§¶а§ѓа§Ња§ња§§(cid:170))
пБґ
а§Єа•Аа§®а•З а§Ѓ(cid:164) а§¶а§¶(cid:170) а§єа•Ла§®а§Њ
пБґ Angina pectoris is the medical
term for chest pain or discomfort
due to coronary heart disease
пБґ It occurs when the heart muscle
doesn't get as much blood as it
needs
пБґ This usually happens because
one or more of the heart's
arteries is narrowed or blocked
пБґ It is also called Ischemia
пБґ Angina is a symptom of coronary
artery disease
пБґ Angina is the chest pain that
occurs due to imbalance between
oxygen supply and demand
пБґ In angina, there is a fixed
atherosclerotic narrowing of the
coronary arteries
16
а§ђа•Аа§Ѓа§Њ(cid:229)а§∞а§ѓа§Ња§В а§Фа§∞ а§Йа§®а§Ха§Њ а§Йа§™а§Ъа§Ња§∞

children.name: CAUSES
children.content:
пБґ Narrowing of blood vessels who supply blood to heart
пБґ Atherosclerosis means buildup of fats, cholesterol and other
substances in and on your artery walls (plaque), which can
restrict blood flow
пБґ Coronary artery disease in which narrowing or blockage of
coronary arteries
пБґ Lack of exercise & Sedentary lifestyle
пБґ Unhealthy diet increase deposit of fat or cholesterol in the
walls of artery and narrow the blood vessels

children.name: SYMPTOMS
children.content:
пБґ Chest pain or discomfort, often described as squeezing
pressure, fullness, tightness, or a heavy weight in the centre of
the chest
пБґ Pain or discomfort in the arms, neck, jaw, shoulder or back
пБґ Pain similar to indigestion or heartburn
пБґ Shortness of breath and tiredness
пБґ Nausea, sweating, and dizziness

children.name: DIAGNOSIS
children.content:
пБґ Electrocardiogram (ECG)
пБґ Exercise stress test
пБґ Echocardiogram
пБґ Chest X-ray
пБґ Coronary angiography
пБґ Computerised tomography (CT) scan
пБґ Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI)
17

вАҐ Isosorbide-5-mononitrate: 20вАУ40 mg oral
вАҐ Erythrityl tetranitrate: 15вАУ60 mg oral

вАҐ Nicorandil: 5вАУ20 mg BD
2. BETA BLOCKERS:
вАҐ Propranolol OralвАФ10 mg BD to 160 mg QID
вАҐ Atenolol: 25 mg ODвАУ50 mg BD
вАҐ Pindolol: 5вАУ15 mg BD By slowing the heart rate, beta blockers
reduce the oxygen demand of the heart and reduce the
frequency of angina attacks in patient with angina pectoris,
Guidelines recommend beta blockers as first-line treatment in
patients with angina either on their own or in combination
with a calcium channel blocker
3. CALCIUM CHANNEL BLOCKERS:
вАҐ Verapamil, diltiazem and long acting DHPs (dihydropyridines)
can be used in angina, Short acting DHPs like Nifedipine
should be avoided because these can accentuate the
symptoms of angina by causing tachycardia

children.content:
пБґ
а§ња§¶а§≤ а§Х(cid:236) а§Іа•Ьа§Ха§®а•З а§Еа§ња§®а§ѓа§ња§Ѓа§§ а§єа•Ла§®а§Њ
пБґ An arrhythmia is a problem with the
rate or rhythm of the heartbeat
пБґ During an arrhythmia, the heart can
beat too fast, too slowly, or with an
irregular rhythm
пБґ A normal resting heart rate for adults
ranges from 60 to 100 beats per
minute, When a heart beats too fast,
more than 100 beats per minute the
condition is called Tachycardia
пБґ When a heart beats too slowly fewer
than 60 beats per minute (BPM)
qualifies as bradycardia, the condition
is called Bradycardia

children.name: CAUSES
children.content:
пБґ A heart attack that's occurring right now
пБґ Scarring of heart tissue from a prior heart attack
пБґ Changes to your heart's structure, such as from Cardiomyopathy
пБґ Blocked arteries in your heart (coronary artery disease)
пБґ High blood pressure
пБґ The most common type of arrhythmia is atrial fibrillation, which
causes an irregular and fast heart beat
пБґ Many factors can affect your heart's rhythm, such as having had
a heart attack, smoking, congenital heart defects, and stress
пБґ Some substances or medicines may also cause arrhythmias
24

children.content:

пБґ Dizziness or lightheadedness sudden cardiac arrest
пБґ Fainting or near-fainting spells пБґ Fluttering chest sensation
пБґ Rapid heartbeat or pounding in пБґ Tiredness
the chest пБґ Lightheaded or dizzy
пБґ Shortness of breath and пБґ Chest pain
anxiety пБґ Short of breath
пБґ Chest pain or pressure пБґ Sweaty

children.name: DIAGNOSIS
children.content:
пБґ The most common test used to
diagnose an arrhythmia is an
electrocardiogram (EKG or ECG)
пБґ She or he may recommend
medicines, placement of a device
that can correct an irregular
heartbeat
пБґ Surgery to repair nerves that are
overstimulating the heart

children.name: TREATMENT OPTIONS
children.content:
пБґ ANTIARRHYTHMIC DRUGS:
пБґ These are drugs used to prevent or treat irregularities of cardiac
rhythm, Arrhythmias are the most important cause of sudden
cardiac death
пБґ However, only few arrhythmias need to be treated with
antiarrhythmic drugs
25

пБґ Verapamil: 5 mg slow i.v. injection over 2вАУ3 min (to terminate

- Ketolorac (10вАУ20 mg oral 6 hourly, 15вАУ30 mg i.m./i.v. 6 hourly (max 90 mg/day))
- Piroxicam: 20 mg BD for two days followed by 20 mg OD

### DISEASE-MODIFYING ANTIRHEUMATIC DRUGS
- Sulfasalazine (1вАУ3 g/day in 2вАУ3 divided doses)
- Methotrexate (7.5вАУ15 mg weekly oral)
- Penicillamine (125-250 mg OD (max 250 mg BD))
- Azathioprine (50вАУ150 mg/day)
- Hydroxychloroquine (initially 200 mg BD followed by 200 mg OD for maintenance)

### ANTI-GOUT DRUGS:
- Allopurinol (Start with 100 mg OD, Maintenance dose of 300 mg/day, maximum 600 mg/day)
- Colchicine (dose 0.5вАУ1 mg/day)
- Febuxostat (40 milligrams (mg) or 80 mg once a day)
- Sulfinpyrazone: 100вАУ200 BD
- Probenecid (0.25вАУ1.0 g BD)

пБґ Asthma is a condition in which your airways narrow and swell
and may produce extra mucus
пБґ Due to this swelling, the air path produces excess mucus making
it hard to breathe.

whistling sound (wheezing) when you breathe out and shortness
of breath
пБґ The disease is chronic and interferes with daily working

children.name: INTRODUCTION
children.content:
пБґ NEOPLASM
пБґ Cancer is a term used for diseases in which abnormal cells
divide without control & are able to invade other tissues
пБґ Cancer cells can spread to other parts of the body through the
blood and lymph systems
пБґ Also called вАЬNeoplasmвАЭ
вАҐ Carcinogens cause cancer:
вАҐ A carcinogen is any substance, radionuclide, or radiation that
promotes carcinogenesis, the formation of cancer
вАҐ This may be due to the ability to damage the genome or to the
disruption of cellular metabolic processes
TYPES
1. Cervical cancer 5. Skin cancer
2. Breast cancer 6. Lung cancer
3. Stomach cancer 7. Colon cancer
4. Lymphoma 8. Prostate cancer
TYPES ON THE BASIS OF ORIGIN
1. Carcinoma 5. Myeloma
2. Sarcoma 6. Blastoma
3. Leukemia 7. Germ cell cancer
4. Lymphoma 8. Central nervous
system cancers
50

children.content:
пБґ Radiation пБґ Enviornment пБґ Occupation

children.content:

sweat) пБґ Bowel Changes (Colon
пБґ Severe Pains cancer)
пБґ Neurological symptoms пБґ Fever(Leukemia and
lymphoma)

пБґ Blood tests (Complete Blood пБґ Magnetic resonance imaging
Count-CBC) (MRI)

Cellular diagnosis test tomography (PET) scan
пБґ X-Ray, MRI scanning пБґ Ultrasound and X-ray, among
(Magnetic resonance others
imaging), CT scan пБґ Biopsy During a biopsy:

children.name: TREATMENT OPTIONS
children.content:
1. Chemotherapy:
пБґ Rapidly dividing cells metastasised cancers (leukemia and
lymphoma)
2. Radiotherapy:
пБґ In combination with other therapies Iodine -131 (thyroid
cancer) Iridium-192 (breast cancer)
3. Surgery :
пБґ Not metastasized cancers but prostate, breast or testicular
cancers
4. Immunotherapy :
пБґ Immune system made strong to fight against cancers
5. Hormone Therapy:
пБґ Killing cancer cells by altering hormone levels
6. Gene Therapy:
пБґ Replacing defective genes
7. Anticancer Drugs:
пБґ These are the class of medication which are use in the
treatment of the various type of cancer either benign or
malignant
пБґ Anticancer also called Antineoplastic Drugs

children.name: MEDICINE OPTIONS
children.content:
1. Mechlorethamine (Mustine HCl): 0.1 mg/kg i.v. daily √Ч 4 days;
courses may be repeated at suitable intervals
2. Cyclophosphamide: 2вАУ3 mg/kg/day oral; 10вАУ15 mg/kg i.v.
every 7вАУ10 days, i.m. use also possible

oxygen and rapidly passes the remaining gas to the large
intestine

children.name: TREATMENT OPTIONS
children.content:
пБґ Several drugs can be used to treat Giardia infection.
пБґ Effective treatments include metronidazole, tinidazole, and
nitazoxanide
пБґ Alternatives to these medications include paromomycin,
quinacrine, and furazolidone

вАҐ 3. Nitazoxanide: 500 mg (children 7.5 mg/kg) BD √Ч 3 days
вАҐ 4. Quiniodochlor:250 mg TDS for 7 days

children.content:
пБґ Shivering пБґ Lack of coordination
пБґ Slurred speech пБґ Drowsiness
пБґ Mumbling пБґ Very low energy
пБґ Slow, shallow breathing пБґ Confusion
пБґ Weak pulse пБґ Memory loss
пБґ Clumsiness пБґ Loss of consciousness

children.name: DIAGNOSIS
children.content:
пБґ The diagnosis of hypothermia is usually apparent based on a
person's physical signs and the conditions in which the person
with hypothermia became ill or was found
пБґ Blood tests also can help confirm hypothermia and its severity
пБґ A diagnosis may not be readily apparent, however,
пБґ If the symptoms are mild, as when an older person who is
indoors has symptoms of confusion, lack of coordination and
speech problems
RISK FACTORS
пБґ Your tolerance for cold diminishes
when you are fatigued.
пБґ Older age. The body's ability to
regulate temperature and to sense
cold may lessen with age
пБґ Very young age
пБґ Mental problems
пБґ Alcohol and drug use
пБґ Medical conditions

пБґ Be gentle, When you're helping a person with hypothermia,
handle him or her gently
пБґ Move the person out of the cold
пБґ Remove wet clothing
пБґ Cover the person with blankets
пБґ Insulate the person's body from the cold ground
пБґ Monitor breathing
пБґ Provide warm beverages
пБґ Dry compresses
пБґ Don't apply direct heat but Passive rewarming
пБґ For someone with mild hypothermia, it is enough to cover
them with heated blankets and offer warm fluids to drink
пБґ Blood rewarming: Warm intravenous fluids
пБґ A warmed intravenous solution of salt water may be put into a
vein to help warm the blood
пБґ Airway rewarming: The use of humidified oxygen
administered with a mask or nasal tube

пБґ Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a disease of the lower GI
Tract, It is characterized by chronic inflammation of the
gastrointestinal (GI) tract and damage it
пБґ Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a term for two conditions
пБґ 1. Crohn's disease 2. Ulcerative colitis
пБґ Both are characterized by chronic inflammation of the
gastrointestinal (GI) tract,
пБґ Prolonged inflammation results in damage to the GI tract
пБґ CROHNвАЩS DISEASE:
пБґ It causes inflammation of digestive tract, which can lead to
abdominal pain, severe diarrhea, fatigue, weight loss and
malnutrition. Can affect any part of the GI tract (from the
mouth to the anus), Most often it affects the portion of the
small intestine before the large intestine/colon
пБґ Damaged areas appear in patches that are next to areas of
healthy tissue

children.name: INTRODUCTION
children.content:
пБґ Visceral leishmaniasis (VL), also
known as kala-azar, is fatal if
left untreated in over 95% of
cases., Word kala-azar consists
of вАЬKalaвАЭ (in Hindi means
вАЬBlackвАЭ) & вАЬazarвАЭ (in Hindi
means вАЬFever)вАЭ
пБґ Fever associated with dark
complexion
пБґ Kala azar, also called visceral
leishmaniasis is a disease in
which a Parasite migrates to the
internal visceral organs such as
the liver, spleen and bone
marrow.
пБґ

children.name: CAUSES
children.content:
пБґ Leishmania donovani (and other Leishmania species) occurs in
several tropical and subtropical regions of the world

children.name: SYMPTOMS
children.content:
пБґ Loss of appetite,Weight loss,
пБґ Fever & Weakness (fatigue)
пБґ Skin вАУ Dry, thin and scaly and hair
may be lost
пБґ Enlarge liver, spleen
пБґ Light colored person show grayish
discoloration of the skin of hands,
feet, abdomen
TREATMENT & MEDICINE OPTIONS
пБґ Option-1 Antimonials
пБґ Sodium stibogluconate (SSG)
(Antimonate), It has been the standard
first line drug for VL in most parts of the
world achieving > 90% cure rate To treat
Kala-azar., Dose: 20 mg Sb/kg/day
(maximum 850 mg) IV/IM for 20-28 days
вАҐ Option-2 Antimonials Meglumine
(Antimonate), Meglumine is an amino
sugar derived from glucose, Injection
Given under medical supervision Dose:
The usual dose is 20 mg of pentavalent
antimony in meglumine antimoniate per
kilogram (kg) of body weight per day
186
а§ђа•Аа§Ѓа§Њ(cid:229)а§∞а§ѓа§Ња§В а§Фа§∞ а§Йа§®а§Ха§Њ а§Йа§™а§Ъа§Ња§∞
вАҐ Option-3 Diamidine: Pentamidine
вАҐ It is an antimicrobial medication Use in
people with poor immune function.
вАҐ Dose: 2 to 4 mg/kg every other day or
three times per week for 15 doses, and
for VL it is 4 mg/kg every other day or
three times per week for 15 to 30 doses.
вАҐ Option-4 Liposomal Amphotericin-B
injection, It is being used increasingly to
reduce the burden of kala-azar from the
Indian subcontinent
вАҐ Dose: 3 mg/kg IV qDay on days 1-5, 14,
and 21
вАҐ Option-5 Miltefosine
вАҐ It is sold under the trade name Impavido
among others, is a medication mainly
used to treat leishmaniasis.
вАҐ Dose: 50 mg orally 3 times a day
вАҐ OTHER TREATMENT OPTIONS IF
PREVIOUS WILL NOT WORK
вАҐ Ketoconazole
вАҐ Paromomycin
вАҐ Allopurinol
вАҐ Some symptoms based medicine also
can be use
вАҐ To reduce fever Paracetamol can be
given to patient.
187

children.name: CAUSES
children.content:
пБґ Bacteria that enter the
bloodstream and travel to the
brain and spinal cord cause acute
bacterial meningitis
пБґ But it can also occur when
bacteria directly invade the
meninges
пБґ This may be caused by an:
пБґ Ear or sinus infection
пБґ A skull fracture
пБґ Rarely вАФ some surgeries

children.content:
пБґ Sudden high fever пБґ Sleepiness or difficulty
пБґ Stiff neck waking
пБґ Severe headache with пБґ Sensitivity to light
nausea or vomiting пБґ No appetite or thirst
пБґ Confusion or difficulty пБґ Skin rash (sometimes, such
concentrating as in meningococcal
пБґ Seizures meningitis)

children.name: DIAGNOSIS
children.content:
пБґ In people with meningitis, the CSF
often shows a
вАҐ Low sugar (glucose) level
вАҐ Along with an increased white
blood cell count
вАҐ Increased protein

children.name: TREATMENT OPTIONS
children.content:
пБґ Acute bacterial meningitis must be treated immediately with
intravenous antibiotics and sometimes corticosteroids
пБґ This helps to ensure recovery and reduce the risk of
complications, such as brain swelling and seizures
пБґ The antibiotic or combination of antibiotics depends on the
type of bacteria causing the infection
пБґ Commonly used meningitis treatments include a class of
antibiotics called cephalosporins, especially Claforan
(cefotaxime) and Rocephin (ceftriaxone)
пБґ Various penicillin-type antibiotics, aminoglycoside drugs such
as gentamicin, and others, are also used

children.content:
пБґ Nausea & vomiting during pregnancy
пБґ Morning sickness is a common symptom of
pregnancy and is marked by nausea and
occasional vomiting, It is called Morning sickness
but it can cause discomfort at any time of the day
пБґ Morning sickness usually happens within the first
four months of pregnancy and is often first sign
that a woman is pregnant

children.content:
пБґ Common signs and symptoms of morning
sickness include nausea and vomiting,
пБґ It enhance by certain odors, spicy foods,
heat, excess salivation
пБґ Morning sickness is most common during
the first trimester and usually begins by
nine weeks after conception, While in some
cases it starts for 4th week of pregnancy &
continue till 16th week of more

PREVENTION
пБґ Avoiding foods and smells that trigger your nausea
пБґ If possible someone other prepare food for you and eat it away
from kitchen so u can eliminate smell and odor
пБґ Allow some time for digestion, and rise slowly once you are ready
пБґ Eating smaller meals more frequently throughout the day instead
of three big meals
пБґ Means take 5 to 6 smaller meal in place of 2-3 big meal
пБґ Drinking less water/fluids with your meals, and instead, drink
them between meals

children.name: INTRODUCTION
children.content:
пБґ Motion sickness is not a disease but a condition when our brain
receives different signals from the inner ear, eye, and skin,
putting the central nervous system in a confusion
пБґ For example, while traveling in a car, give your ears a message
of speed & eyes to read a book
пБґ Motion sickness is a sensation of Drowsiness
пБґ It usually occurs when you're traveling by car, boat, plane, or
train
пБґ Your body's sensory organs send mixed messages to brain,
causing dizziness, lightheadedness, or nausea
пБґ

children.name: CAUSES
children.content:
пБґ Mucormycosis is a fungal
infection caused by certain types
of molds (mucormycetes)
пБґ The most common types that
cause mucormycosis are Rhizopus
species and Mucor species.

children.name: SYMPTOMS
children.content:
пБґ Eyes that swell and stick out пБґ Sinus pain or congestion
(protrude) (а§Жа§Ба§Ца•З (cid:876)а§Ъа§™а§Ха§®а§Њ)
пБґ Coughing blood
пБґ Dark scabbing in nasal пБґ One-sided facial swelling
cavities
пБґ Shortness of breath
пБґ Fever & Headache
пБґ Abdominal pain
пБґ Mental status changes
пБґ Blood in the stools
пБґ Redness of skin above
пБґ Diarrhea
sinuses

children.name: DIAGNOSIS
children.content:
пБґ See an ear-nose-throat (ENT)
пБґ Testing depends on your symptoms,
пБґ but may include these imaging tests:
пБґ CT scans
пБґ MRI scans
пБґ A biopsy must be done to diagnose mucormycosis
пБґ A biopsy is the removal of a small piece of tissue for laboratory
examination to identify the fungus

Maintenance dose: 100 mg orally once a day

then 4 mg/kg 12 hourly. Drug is to be reconstituted and

пБґ Malaria is transmitted by infected mosquitoes
пБґ When you get bitten by a mosquito which carries the malaria
parasite, the parasite enters your bloodstream
пБґ It is then carried to your liver, where it multiplies
TYPES
1. Plasmodium 3. Plasmodium Ovale
Falciparum
4. Plasmodium Malariae
2. Plasmodium Vivax
5. Plasmodium Knowlesi
SYMPTOMS
пБґ Fever пБґ Nausea пБґ Rapid breathing
пБґ Chills пБґ Vomiting пБґ Rapid heart rate
пБґ General feeling of пБґ Muscle or joint пБґ Cough
discomfort pain пБґ Diarrhea
пБґ Headache пБґ Fatigue пБґ Abdominal pain
PREVENTION
пБґ Mosquito repellent пБґ Wear full sleave cloth
пБґ Mosquito repellent Cream пБґ Keep surrounding area clean
пБґ Mosquito Net/Machardani пБґ Maintain good hygiene

пБґ Microscopic Examination of пБґ Polymerase Chain Reaction

пБґ Rapid Diagnostic Tests (RDTs) (QBC) Test
TREATMENT & MEDICINE OPTIONS
1. CHLOROQUINE: 1000 milligrams (mg) once a day, taken for 2
days, Followed by 500 mg once a day for at least 2 to 3 weeks.
2. Artemether-lumefantrine: 3 Days course of total 24 tablets in
six doses (4 Tablet BD for 3 days)
3. Atovaquone-proguanil: 50 milligrams (mg) of atovaquone and
100 mg proguanil (1 adult strength tablet) per day, starting 1 to
2 days before entering malarial area and continuing for 7 days
following return.
4. Primaquine phosphate: For radical cure of vivax malaria: 15 mg
(children 0.25 mg/kg) daily for 2 weeks along with chloroquine
for 3 days; As gametocidal for falciparum malaria 45 mg (0.75
mg/kg) single dose along with chloroquine;
5. Himalaya Platenza: Take 1 to 2 tablets twice daily
6. Resochin: Dose:1 or 2 tablet per day
7. Norsunate-200: Dose: 1 tablet per day
8. Quinestar: Dose: 1 tablet OD/BD
9. Lumerax-60: Dose: 1 tablet per day
10. Lumerax-80: Dose: 1 tablet per day
11. Larinate-100 kit: Single time kit
12. Larinate-200 kit : Single time kit
13. Synriam tablet: 1 tablet per day

children.name: INTRODUCTION
children.content:
пБґ It is the medical condition in which pressure inside the eyes
increase, In medical term High pressure in the eyes is called the
ocular hypertension (INCREASED EYE PRESSURE)
пБґ The front part of the eye is filled with a clear fluid (called
aqueous humor) made by the ciliary body
пБґ Intraocular pressure (IOP):
пБґ Normal eye pressure ranges from 12-22 mm Hg
пБґ Eye pressure of greater than 22 mm Hg is considered higher
than normal Called ocular hypertension
пБґ Ocular hypertension occurs when the pressure in the eye
(known as intraocular pressure) is above the range considered
normal (often defined as above 21 mm Hg)

MEDICINES OPTIONS
пБґ PAIN RELIEVERS:
вАҐ Dicyclomine: 10вАУ20 mg oral.
вАҐ Mefenamic Acid: 250вАУ500 mg TDS
вАҐ Ibuprofen: 400вАУ800 mg TDS
вАҐ Paracetamol: 0.5вАУ1 g TDS
вАҐ Dose: infants 50 mg; children 15 mg/kg (1вАУ3 years 80вАУ160 mg,
4вАУ8 years 240вАУ320 mg, 9вАУ12 years 300вАУ600 mg);

TREATMENT OPTIONS
пБґ HORMONE TREATMENTS:
вАҐ If reason related to female hormonal imbalance then Birth
control pills or hormonal medicines may prescribe
вАҐ Progesterone: 10вАУ100 mg i.m. (as oily solution) OD
вАҐ Medroxyprogesterone acetate: 5вАУ20 mg ODвАУBD oral,
вАҐ Norethindrone (Norethisterone): 5вАУ10 mg ODвАУBD oral
вАҐ Levonorgestrel: 0.1вАУ0.5 mg/day
пБґ ANTIBIOTICS: If pain due to bacterial infection in stomach
вАҐ Levofloxacin: 500 mg OD oral, 500 mg by slow i.v. infusion
вАҐ Ofloxacin: 200вАУ400 mg BD oral, 200 mg by slow i.v. infusion
вАҐ Cefadroxil: 0.5вАУ1 g BD
пБґ ANTIDEPRESSANTS: If reason is Anxiety of fear
вАҐ 0.25вАУ1.0 mg TDS for anxiety
пБґ MUSCLE RELAXERS:
вАҐ Cyclobenzaprine is a centrally acting skeletal muscle relaxant
вАҐ It help to prevent pelvic pain by relaxing muscles in pelvic
area Dose: 10 milligrams (mg) 3 times a day
пБґ HOME REMEDIES
пБґ Regular exercise
пБґ Hot water bag massage
пБґ Avoid alcohol
пБґ Avoid smoking
пБґ Take healthy food
пБґ Manage stress level
пБґ Multivitamin-multi
mineral supplements

пБґ Pelvic inflammatory disease (PID) is defined as an inflammation
of the upper genital tract due to an infection in women. The
disease affects the uterus, fallopian tubes, and/or ovaries
пБґ PID can become extremely dangerous, even life-threatening, if
the infection spreads into the blood

lower tummy пБґ Painful periods
пБґ Discomfort or pain during sex пБґ Unusual vaginal discharge,
that's felt deep inside the
especially if it's yellow, green
pelvis
or smelly
пБґ Pain when peeing пБґ Severe pain in the tummy
пБґ Bleeding between periods пБґ A high temperature (fever)

children.name: DIAGNOSIS
children.content:
пБґ PELVIC EXAM TO CHECK PELVIC ORGANS
пБґ Cervical culture to check cervix for infections
пБґ Urine test to check your urine for signs of blood, cancer, and
other diseases, After collecting samples, your doctor sends
these samples to a laboratory
пБґ ASSESSING DAMAGE
пБґ If your doctor determines that you have pelvic inflammatory
disease, they may run more tests and check your pelvic area for
damage. PID can cause scarring on your fallopian tubes and
permanent damage to your reproductive organs
пБґ PELVIC ULTRASOUND: This is an imaging test that uses sound
waves to create pictures of your internal organs
пБґ ENDOMETRIAL BIOPSY: In this outpatient procedure a doctor
removes and examines a small sample from the lining of your
uterus
пБґ LAPAROSCOPY: A laparoscopy is an outpatient procedure
where a doctor inserts a flexible instrument through an incision
in your abdomen and takes pictures of your pelvic organs
242
а§ђа•Аа§Ѓа§Њ(cid:229)а§∞а§ѓа§Ња§В а§Фа§∞ а§Йа§®а§Ха§Њ а§Йа§™а§Ъа§Ња§∞

children.name: TREATMENT OPTIONS
children.content:
1. Empiric treatment for PID in the inpatient setting includes:
вАҐ Ceftriaxone 1 g IV every 24 hours PLUS Doxycycline 100 mg
orally or IV every 12 hours PLUS Metronidazole 500 mg orally
or IV every 12 hours
вАҐ Cefotetan (2 g intravenously [IV] every 12 hours) plus
doxycycline (100 mg by mouth every 12 hours) or
вАҐ Cefoxitin (2 g IV every 6 hours) plus doxycycline (100 mg by
mouth every 12 hours) or
вАҐ Clindamycin (900 mg IV every 8 hours) plus gentamicin (3 to 5
mg/kg IV once daily)
2. The CDC recommends the following for first-line treatment for
outpatient therapy:
вАҐ Doxycycline (100 mg orally twice a day for 2 weeks) plus
ceftriaxone 500 mg intramuscularly (IM) for one dose or
cefoxitin 2 g IM with probenecid (1g orally) for one dose or
another parenteral third-generation cephalosporin
вАҐ Metronidazole (500 mg orally twice per day for 14 days) should
be added if there is a concern for trichomonas or recent vaginal
instrumentation
243

pathogenesis of ulcer disease: lifetime prevalence of
developing ulcer disease in first-degree relatives of ulcer
patients is about three times greater than the general
population (Agar family me kisi ko past me ulcer hua tha)

the age group 12вАУ45 years, In PCOD multiple bubble-like form
on the surface of one or both of ovaries, Due to presence of
multiple cyst eggs partially mature but are not released, These
eggs remain in their follicles, which swell but don't open
пБґ Thus it cause irregular menstrual cycle in women
пБґ It can cause problems with menstrual periods and make it
difficult for female to conceive Pregnancy
пБґ It cause irregular or prolonged menstrual periods, and often
excess male hormone (androgen) levels

children.name: CAUSES
children.content:
пБґ The cause of PCOD is still
unknown
пБґ But following reasons are
responsible for
PCOD/PCOS

and hormonal imbalance
пБґ PCOD with low-grade

а§ђа•Аа§Ѓа§Њ(cid:229)а§∞а§ѓа§Ња§В а§Фа§∞ а§Йа§®а§Ха§Њ а§Йа§™а§Ъа§Ња§∞
пБґ Production of male hormones (hyperandrogenism) in high
quantity and genetics can be found
пБґ Also, early age of menarche, unhealthy lifestyle and pollution
are some of the contributing factors of PCOD
пБґ Insulin resistance means the body's tissues are resistant to the
effects of insulin. The body therefore has to produce extra
insulin to compensate
пБґ High levels of insulin causes the ovaries to produce too much
testosterone, which interferes with the development of the
follicles (the sacs in the ovaries where eggs develop) and
prevents normal ovulation
пБґ It also lead to weight gain, which can make PCOS symptoms
worse, as having excess fat causes body to produce more
insulin
пБґ Production of male hormones (hyperandrogenism) in high
quantity and genetics can be found

PREVENTION
пБґ Washing your hands regularly and thoroughly, particularly after
touching your nose or mouth, and before handling food.
пБґ Sneezing and coughing into tissues.
пБґ This will help prevent the virus-or bacteria containing droplets
from your nose and mouth entering the air where they can
infect others

пБґ Rheumatoid arthritis is a long-term autoimmune disorder that
primarily affects joints, It typically results in warm, swollen, and
painful joints, It is a chronic inflammatory disorder that can
affect more than just your joints
пБґ In some people, condition can damage a wide variety of body
systems, including the skin, eyes, lungs, heart and blood vessels
пБґ An autoimmune disorder, rheumatoid arthritis occurs when your
immune system mistakenly attacks your own body's tissues,
Damage the tissue and after then mace inflammation of that
tissue , When inflammation occurs around joints condition
becomes painful

children.name: INTRODUCTION
children.content:
Schizophrenia is a serious mental disorder in which people interpret
reality abnormally, Schizophrenia may result in some combination of
hallucinations, delusions, and extremely disordered thinking and
behavior that impairs daily functioning, and can be disabling

children.name: TREATMENT OPTIONS
children.content:
1. Treatment with medications and psychosocial therapy can
help manage the condition, In some cases, hospitalization.
2. Medications are the cornerstone of schizophrenia treatment,
and ANTIPSYCHOTIC MEDICATIONS are the most commonly
prescribed drugs Treatment with medications and
psychosocial therapy can help manage the condition
3. Medications are the cornerstone of schizophrenia treatment,
and antipsychotic medications are the most commonly
prescribed drugs
4. Training primary health-care personnel
5. Providing access to essential drugs
6. Supporting families in providing home care
7. Educating the public to decrease stigma and discrimination
8. Enhancing independent living skills through recovery-oriented
psychosocial interventions (e.g. Life skills training, social skills
training)

children.name: DIAGNOSIS
children.content:
пБґ The Mantoux tuberculin skin test
(TST) or the, Mantoux tuberculin skin
test is performed by injecting a small
amount of fluid called tuberculin
into the skin in the lower part of the
arm, The test is done by putting a
small amount of TB protein
(antigens) under the top layer of skin
on your inner forearm
пБґ If you have ever been exposed to the
TB bacteria your skin will react to
the antigens by developing a firm
red bump at the site within 2 or 3
days. Indicates positive test

children.content:
пБґ Fever that starts low and пБґ Dry cough
increases daily, reach high as пБґ Loss of appetite
104.9 ¬∞F Headache

пБґ Weakness
пБґ Diarrhea or constipation

children.name: DIAGNOSIS
children.content:
пБґ Widal test is the main confirmative test for Typhoid Disease
пБґ Also perform blood test to check blood counts & Hb
пБґ Different vaccines available to prevent typhoid
пБґ Vivotif is one of them (Typhoid Vaccine Live
Oral Ty21a), Also known as 'typhoid pillsвАЩ,
пБґ It is made from attenuated live bacteria.
пБґ The vaccine provides up to five years'
protection and is approved for use in
individuals over six-years-old
пБґ Typhoid Vaccination Schedule:
пБґ Typhoid conjugate vaccines (Vi-PS):
пБґ Typbar-TCV¬Ѓ: Single dose at 9-12 through 23
months followed by a booster at 2 years of age
пБґ Pedatyph¬Ѓ : Single dose at 9-12 through 23
months followed by a booster at 2 years of age

children.name: TREATMENT OPTIONS
children.content:
пБґ Drink plenty of fluids (Pani Jyada Pina chahiye)
пБґ Need to be hydrated during the treatment of Typhoid fever
пБґ So take water properly
пБґ 1. Drink more water in a day must take 5 to 7 littre/day water
пБґ 2. Be hydrated
пБґ 3. Limit junk food in diet
пБґ Antibiotics therapy & other medicines :
пБґ The only effective treatment for typhoid is antibiotics
пБґ The most commonly used are ciprofloxacin (for non-pregnant
adults) and ceftriaxone
пБґ Coconut water, fresh fruit juices are also best options
пБґ Lemon juice and orange are also given to patient.

пБґ Vertigo generally describe the sensation as вАЬfeeling dizzyвАЭ, it is a
sensation of feeling off balance

spinning or that the world around you is spinning
пБґ It can happen when there is a problem with: The inner ear,
Brain, Sensory nerve pathway.
пБґ Vertigo is the feeling that you are moving when you are not Or it
might feel like things around you are moving when they are not

children.name: INTRODUCTION
children.content:
пБґ Viral Infection caused by the presence of virus in body
пБґ Viral infections are any illness you get from a virus (a small
germ that uses your cells to reproduce).
пБґ Common viral illnesses include colds, the flu, COVID-19,
norovirus (вАЬstomach fluвАЭ), HPV (warts) and herpes simplex virus
(cold sores).

пБґ For most viral infections, treatments can only help with
symptoms while you wait for your immune system to fight off
the virus, Antibiotics do not work for viral infections
пБґ There are antiviral medicines to treat some viral infections
пБґ Vaccines can help prevent you from getting many viral diseases
пБґ Symptoms based treatment also we can provide
пБґ If fever then we can control fever using paracetamol

а§ђа•Аа§Ѓа§Њ(cid:229)а§∞а§ѓа§Ња§В а§Фа§∞ а§Йа§®а§Ха§Њ а§Йа§™а§Ъа§Ња§∞
TREATMENT AND MEDICINE OPTIONS
пБґ If symptoms of cough then we can give medicines for cough
and cold
пБґ If symptoms are body pain and headache then we can treat
patient using pain relievers (analgesic)
пБґ Then based on diagnosis proper antiviral medicine based on
the type of virus
пБґ Antiviral drugs are a class of medication used specifically for
treating viral infections rather than bacterial ones
пБґ Most antivirals are used for specific viral infections, while a
broad-spectrum antiviral is effective against a wide range of
viruses
COMBINATION THERAPY FROM TREATMENT OF VIRAL INFECTION
пБґ 1. Lamivudine 150 mg + Zidovudine 300 mg tab (1 tab BD);
пБґ 2. Lamivudine 150 mg + Stavudine 30 mg or 40 mg tab (1BD)
пБґ 3. Lamivudine 150 mg + Zidovudine 300 mg + Nevirapine
пБґ 4. Lamivudine 150 mg + Stavudine 30 mg or 40 mg
+Nevirapine 200 mg tab (1 tab BD)
пБґ 5. Lamivudine 150 mg + Zidovudine 300 mg 2 tab and
Efavirenz 600 mg 1 tab kit
OTHER ANTIVIRAL MEDICINE
пБґ Acyclovir: 200 mg 5 times a day oral (15 mg/kg/day), 5вАУ10 mg/
kg 8 hourly by slow i.v. infusion, 5% topical Cream 6 times/day
пБґ Valaciclovir: For genital herpes simplex 0.5-1.0 g BD 7-10 days
пБґ Zidovudine (Azidothymidine, AZT): Adults 300 mg BD
пБґ Nevirapine: 200 mg/day oral
пБґ Efavirenz: 600 mg OD on empty stomach
пБґ Lamivudine: For chronic hepatitis BвАФ100 mg OD, For HIV
infectionвАФ150 mg BD (with other antiretroviral drugs)
пБґ Oseltamivir: therapeutic doseвАФ75 mg BD
пБґ Indinavir: 800 mg TDS

пБґ Emesis means the action or
process of vomiting
пБґ Vomiting can have causes that
aren't due to underlying disease
пБґ Examples include hangover,
pregnancy, overeating or motion
sickness, food poison

delicate bones that transmit sound vibrations from your
eardrum to your inner ear so you can hear

MEDICINES
1. Angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitor
пБґ This medications Relaxed blood vessel and reduce blood
pressure in patient with CKD examples are
пБґ Captopril :25 mg BD, increase upto 50 mg TDS as needed
пБґ Enalapril: 2.5 mg ODвАУ20 mg BD
пБґ Lisinopril: 5 mg ODвАУ20 mg BD
2. Angiotensin receptor blocker (ARB)
пБґ This are second options to reduce BP in CKD patients
пБґ Irbesartan: 150вАУ300 mg OD
пБґ Valsartan: 80вАУ160 mg OD
пБґ Losartan: 50 mg OD (max. 50 mg BD
пБґ Olmesartan: 20-40 mg OD
пБґ Telmisartan: 20вАУ80 mg OD
3. Diuretics for CKD
пБґ Loop diuretics are more preferred to reduce BP in CKD patient
with GFR Less than 30 mL/min/1.73m2
пБґ Furosemide: Usually 20вАУ80 mg once daily in the morning
пБґ Bumetanide: 1вАУ5 mg oral once daily in the morning
пБґ Torsemide: 2.5вАУ20 mg once daily in the morning
4. Erythropoietin: Weekly 1 Injection
5. Vitamin D: 1 sachet/granules per week

children.content:
пБґ Hemorrhoids or PILES
пБґ Haemorrhoids, also known as piles, are swellings containing
enlarged blood vessels that are found inside or around the
bottom (the rectum and anus).
пБґ Hemorrhoids is a disease which is very painful.
пБґ These swollen veins inside of your rectum or outside of your
anus can cause pain, anal itching and rectal bleeding
вАҐ In this problem swelling of veins in the anus and lower rectum.
вАҐ They can develop inside or outside the anus
вАҐ 1. Internal hemorrhoid
вАҐ 2. External hemorrhoid

пБґ Herbal and homeopathic cream options
пБґ SBL Ami visnaga cream
350
а§ђа•Аа§Ѓа§Њ(cid:229)а§∞а§ѓа§Ња§В а§Фа§∞ а§Йа§®а§Ха§Њ а§Йа§™а§Ъа§Ња§∞
TREATMENT OPTION
пБґ Medications: No drug can stop the process of vitiligo вАФ the
loss of pigment cells (melanocytes). But some drugs, used
alone, in combination or with light therapy, can help restore
some color2.
пБґ Corticosteroids: Applying a corticosteroid cream to affected
skin might return color. This is most effective when vitiligo is
still in its early stages. (Betnovate cream)
пБґ Calcineurin inhibitors: These include pimecrolimus cream and
tacrolimus ointment. They are often used on the eyelids, face,
neck, armpits, or groin, as these areas may be more prone to
side effects from topical corticosteroids1.
пБґ Light therapy: Phototherapy with narrow band ultraviolet B
(UVB) has been shown to stop or slow the progression of
active vitiligo. It might be more effective when used with
corticosteroids or calcineurin inhibitors2.
пБґ Skin grafting: If light therapy or laser treatments donвАЩt work, a
dermatologist may recommend treating vitiligo with skin
grafting1.
пБґ Cell transplant: This is another option if other treatments
donвАЩt work1.
пБґ Cosmetic cover-ups: Some people choose not to treat their
vitiligo and instead use cosmetic cover-ups
Apply 2-3 times a day Apply 2-3 times a day
